rationale: Reflection/redraft bounded-termination catalog (QA-EVAL-R2.11 / EVAL-R7). Three adversarial fixtures drive the PRODUCTION graph (build_graph) into each pathological recovery loop and assert it terminates GRACEFULLY at the matching monotonic bound with a DEGRADED status -- NEVER an unbounded loop, NEVER an error, and NEVER budget_exceeded (which is reserved for a refused cost admission, COST-R4, and must not be reachable by a coverage/redraft/replan loop). The cost-admission gate ALWAYS admits in these fixtures, so a budget_exceeded outcome here would be a real defect, not a designed exit. QA-EVAL-R6 termination gate: 100% of fixtures terminate at their bound and degrade.

cases.rationale: The coverage assessor reports an open gap on every pass and reflect always REPLANs, so the assess_coverage -> reflect -> plan_retrieval cycle would loop forever; it MUST instead spend the reflection budget to MAX_REFLECTIONS and degrade. Asserts status==degraded, reflection_count==MAX_REFLECTIONS, status NOT budget_exceeded.

The grounder asks to REGENERATE on every pass, so the ground -> compose redraft cycle would loop forever; it MUST instead spend the redraft budget to MAX_REDRAFTS and degrade. Asserts status==degraded, redraft_count==MAX_REDRAFTS, status NOT budget_exceeded.

The grounder asks to REPLAN on every pass, so the ground -> reflect -> plan_retrieval re-plan cycle would loop forever; it MUST instead spend the reflection budget to MAX_REFLECTIONS and degrade. This is the distinct third recovery cycle (GRAPH-R3) and proves a ground-driven loop also terminates. Asserts status==degraded, reflection_count==MAX_REFLECTIONS, status NOT budget_exceeded.
